  - Maintains the methoxy and fluorophenyl, which enhance drug-likeness (QED).
  - Amide linked to a 2-hydroxyethyl group (NCCO), a small polar side chain that may improve solubility and binding without increasing SA.
  - Smaller and easier to synthesize than previous structures, potentially lowering SA.
  - Expected better QED from retention of desirable groups.
  - Potentially improved binding due to polar hydroxyethyl substitution.

Thus this molecule balances drug-likeness (QED), ease of synthesis (SA), and likely improves binding (c-MET score).
